2604.25846.title: Towards Agentic Investigation of Security Alerts
2604.25846.paper_summary: Security analysts are overwhelmed by the volume of alerts and the low context provided by many detection systems. Early-stage investigations typically require manual correlation across multiple log sources, a task that is usually time-consuming. In this paper, we present an experimental, agentic workflow that leverages large language models (LLMs) augmented with predefined queries and constrained tool access (structured SQL over Suricata logs and grep-based text search) to automate the first stages of alert investigation. The proposed workflow integrates queries to provide an overview of the available data, and LLM components that selects which queries to use based on the overview results, extracts raw evidence from the query results, and delivers a final verdict of the alert. Our results demonstrate that the LLM-powered workflow can investigate log sources, plan an investigation, and produce a final verdict that has a significantly higher accuracy than a verdict produced by the same LLM without the proposed workflow. By recognizing the inherent limitations of directly applying LLMs to high-volume and unstructured data, we propose combining existing investigation practices of real-world analysts with a structured approach to leverage LLMs as virtual security analysts, thereby assisting and reducing the manual workload.
2604.25846.paper_summary_zh: 安全分析師面對著大量的警報和許多檢測系統提供的低上下文而感到不堪重負。早期的調查通常需要在多個日誌來源之間進行手動關聯，這是一項通常耗時的任務。在本文中，我們提出了一種實驗性的、主動的工作流程，利用大型語言模型（LLMs），並輔以預定義查詢和受限工具訪問（針對Suricata日誌的結構化SQL和基於grep的文本搜索），以自動化警報調查的初始階段。所提出的工作流程整合了查詢，以提供可用數據的概覽，並且LLM組件根據概覽結果選擇使用哪些查詢，從查詢結果中提取原始證據，並給出警報的最終判斷。我們的結果表明，基於LLM的工作流程可以調查日誌來源，計劃調查，並產生一個最終判斷，其準確性顯著高於同一LLM在未使用所提出工作流程的情況下產生的判斷。通過認識到直接將LLM應用於高容量和非結構化數據的固有限制，我們建議將現實世界分析師的現有調查實踐與結構化方法相結合，以利用LLM作為虛擬安全分析師，從而協助並減少手動工作量。
2604.25846.author: Even Eilertsen et.al.
2604.25846.authors: Even Eilertsen, Vasileios Mavroeidis, Gudmund Grov
